mission.title: Install and Configure Flux on Kubernetes
mission.description: Flux is an open and extensible continuous delivery solution for Kubernetes, powered by the GitOps Toolkit. Installing Flux allows you to keep your Kubernetes clusters in sync with sources of configuration, automating updates to configuration when there is new code to deploy.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Bitnami Helm repository to your Helm configuration to access the Flux chart.
```bash
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Flux using Helm
steps.description:
Install Flux in the 'flux-system' namespace using the specified version.
```bash
helm install flux bitnami/flux --namespace flux-system --create-namespace --version 2.4.36
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Flux pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace flux-system
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Flux deployment to ensure it runs efficiently.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment flux --namespace flux-system --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources", "value": {"limits": {"cpu": "500m", "memory": "512Mi"}, "requests": {"cpu": "250m", "memory": "256Mi"}}}]'
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Flux release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall flux --namespace flux-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any custom resource definitions (CRDs) created by Flux.
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep flux | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'flux-system' namespace to clean up any remaining resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace flux-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current Flux configuration for backup purposes.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace flux-system -o yaml > flux-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure the Helm repository is up to date before upgrading.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Flux
upgrade.description:
Upgrade Flux to the latest version available in the Bitnami repository.
```bash
helm upgrade flux bitnami/flux --namespace flux-system --version 2.4.36
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Flux pods are running the new version successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace flux-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you see Flux pods in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace flux-system
``` 
Common issues include misconfigured secrets or incorrect resource limits.

troubleshooting.title: Flux not syncing with Git repository
troubleshooting.description:
If Flux is not syncing, verify the Git repository configuration:
```bash
kubectl get gitrepositories --namespace flux-system
``` 
Ensure the repository URL and credentials are correct.

troubleshooting.title: Helm release failed
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm release fails, check the events in the namespace:
```bash
kubectl get events --namespace flux-system
``` 
This may indicate issues with resource availability or permissions.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Flux will have all pods running in the 'flux-system' namespace without errors. You should also see Flux actively syncing with your configured Git repository.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
